page: Amazon Web Services m6id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores using Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the middle 50% average. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, contributing to top-tier performance in Passmark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks are strong, though the overall Passmark memory score remains in the average tier.

The instance offers high resource density by combining substantial compute capacity with large local NVMe storage. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, avoiding the network latency and cost of external block storage. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale LLM training, though it achieves strong results in prompt processing for small and medium LLMs. It is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz. It includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution, while single-core metrics remain in the average tier. The instance lacks GPU acceleration but delivers strong database operations and software compilation speeds. It is suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and software development workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose platform combining high-capacity local NVMe storage with 128 vCPUs for data-intensive and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks
Strong multi-core processing, database operations, and software compilation
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
